title: Gymnadenia × borelii
taxonomy: Gymnadenia × borelii is a hybrid orchid species, and as such, its classification is unique. The species is a cross between Gymnadenia conopsea and Gymnadenia densiflora, two other species within the Orchidaceae family. This hybrid classification affects its nomenclature, as it does not fit into the traditional binomial system used for distinct species. As a hybrid, Gymnadenia × borelii is often considered a nothospecies or a hybrid species within the Orchidaceae family. The classification of Gymnadenia × borelii highlights the complexities of orchid taxonomy.
uses: Gymnadenia × borelii, a hybrid orchid, is reportedly used in traditional medicine in some European cultures. However, there is limited scientific research on its medicinal properties. The plant's roots are sometimes used to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and gastrointestinal problems. Additionally, it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. More studies are needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Gymnadenia × borelii for medicinal purposes.

preparations: Gymnadenia × borelii is primarily used in traditional folk medicine in Europe, where it is valued for its potential anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties. intro: Gymnadenia × borelii, also known as Borelli's orchid, is a hybrid orchid species found in the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and boosting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Gymnadenia × borelii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions, tinctures, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin problems.
